DESCRIPTION: Apple Inc. Annual Shareholders Meeting. Agenda includes board elections, executive compensation advisory vote, and shareholder proposals on privacy and supply chain transparency.
Microsoft Annual Shareholders Meeting. Proposals include director elections, Say-on-Pay, and an advisory vote on AI ethics governance.
NVIDIA Annual Meeting of Stockholders. Key votes include board member elections, ratification of PwC as auditor, and shareholder resolutions on AI safety disclosure.
Broadcom Inc. Annual General Meeting. Items include director elections, auditor ratification, and advisory vote on executive compensation following VMware integration.
AMD Annual Meeting of Stockholders. Shareholders will vote on board elections, compensation policies, and a proposal to expand equity incentive plan for AI chip R&D talent retention.
ASML Annual General Meeting of Shareholders. Agenda covers financial results approval, dividend proposal of €6.80/share, Supervisory Board elections, and EUV roadmap discussion.
Texas Instruments Annual Meeting of Stockholders. Shareholders vote on board elections, executive pay, and capital allocation strategy including analog chip fab expansion.
Applied Materials reports Q1 FY2025 preliminary revenue of $7.17B, up 7% YoY, driven by Gate-All-Around (GAA) patterning systems demand and advanced packaging equipment orders from leading foundries.
NVIDIA releases preliminary Q1 FY2026 revenue of $44.1B, exceeding consensus of $43.3B. Data center revenue of $39.3B reflects continued H200/B200 GPU demand surge from hyperscaler AI buildout.
AMD Q1 2025 revenue preliminary report: $7.44B total revenue, MI300X AI GPU revenue reached $3.9B. Data Center segment grew 57% YoY as cloud customers ramp EPYC server deployments.
ASML Q1 2025 revenue of €7.74B, net bookings €3.94B including 3 High-NA EUV systems. Management reiterates FY2025 guidance of €30–35B as leading-edge logic demand remains robust.
Qualcomm Q2 FY2025 revenue of $10.84B, ahead of guidance. Handset chip revenue grew 8% as Snapdragon 8 Elite wins expanded; automotive revenues set a new record at $959M.
Texas Instruments Q1 2025 revenue of $4.07B, up 11% YoY. Industrial and automotive segments recover; management guides Q2 revenue $4.17–4.53B as inventory correction bottoms out.
Lam Research Q3 FY2025 revenue of $4.72B, up 24% YoY. NAND equipment shipments accelerate as major memory manufacturers ramp 200+ layer NAND; etch and deposition tool bookings set record high.
Broadcom declares quarterly cash dividend of $0.59 per share on common stock, payable May 2, 2025 to shareholders of record as of April 22, 2025. Annualized yield ~1.3%.
Texas Instruments goes ex-dividend for $1.36 per share quarterly dividend. Payable May 13, 2025. Annualized yield ~2.8%. 21st consecutive year of dividend increases.
Qualcomm declares quarterly dividend of $0.87 per common share, payable June 26, 2025, to stockholders of record at close of business June 5, 2025.
Applied Materials ex-dividend date for $0.40 per share quarterly cash dividend. Payable May 23, 2025, to shareholders of record April 18, 2025.
KLA Corporation declares quarterly dividend of $1.70 per share, payable June 2, 2025. Yield ~0.9%. KLA has grown its dividend at a 15% CAGR over the past 5 years.
Microsoft goes ex-dividend for $0.83 per share quarterly dividend. Payable June 12, 2025, to shareholders of record May 15, 2025. 14th consecutive annual dividend increase.
Apple Inc. confirms Q2 FY2025 earnings release date of May 1, 2025 after market close, with live earnings conference call at 5:00 PM ET hosted by CFO Kevan Parekh.
Microsoft confirms FY2025 Q3 earnings call for April 30, 2025, 5:30 PM ET. Management will discuss Azure AI growth, Copilot monetization, and guidance for Q4 FY2025.
NVIDIA confirms Q1 FY2026 earnings date of May 28, 2025, after market close. Blackwell GPU ramp and data center margin expansion will be the key focus of the call.
ASML confirms Q1 2025 earnings call for April 16, 2025 at 9:00 AM CET, hosted by CEO Christophe Fouquet and CFO Roger Dassen. High-NA EUV system deliveries to be highlighted.
AMD confirms Q1 2025 earnings date of April 29, 2025, 2:00 PM PT. Lisa Su will discuss MI300X/MI350 shipments, EPYC server ramp, and updated AI GPU revenue guidance.
Broadcom confirms Q2 FY2025 earnings date of June 12, 2025 after market close. Custom ASIC (XPU) revenue and VMware integration progress will be primary discussion topics.
Qualcomm confirms Q3 FY2025 earnings date of July 23, 2025 after market close. Automotive revenue trajectory and Snapdragon X PC market share gains expected to dominate Q&A.

NVIDIA presents at Bernstein Strategic Decisions Technology Conference 2025. CFO Colette Kress to outline AI infrastructure investment cycle, Blackwell supply ramp, and software monetization through CUDA ecosystem.
Apple presents at Goldman Sachs Technology and Internet Conference 2025. Discussion topics include iPhone 17 production ramp, Apple Intelligence monetization, and India manufacturing expansion.
ASML at Jefferies Semiconductor Equipment Forum 2025. CTO Martin van den Brink presents roadmap for High-NA EUV lithography and 2nm/1.4nm process node timelines.
AMD presents at Morgan Stanley Technology, Media & Telecom Conference 2025. Lisa Su to discuss AI GPU competitive positioning vs. NVIDIA, MI350X timeline, and software/ecosystem investments.
Broadcom presents at Citi Global Technology Conference 2025. Hock Tan discusses custom ASIC XPU opportunities with hyperscaler customers, VMware integration synergies, and capital allocation plans.
Micron presents at Credit Suisse Technology Crossover Summit 2025. CEO Sanjay Mehrotra to discuss HBM4 demand ramp for AI accelerators, DRAM pricing recovery, and 2026 capacity plans.
Qualcomm AI Day 2025 — dedicated analyst conference covering Snapdragon AI hub, on-device LLM inference capabilities, automotive cockpit SoC roadmap, and XR ecosystem expansion.
ASML Q1 2025 earnings: Revenue €7.74B (+22% YoY), net income €2.36B, EPS €6.00 (beat €5.78 estimate). Net bookings €3.94B, gross margin 54.0%. FY2025 guidance maintained at €30–35B revenue.
Lam Research Q3 FY2025 earnings: Revenue $4.72B (+24% YoY), EPS $1.04 (beat $0.96 estimate). NAND equipment recovery accelerates; operating margin expanded to 30.2%. Q4 guidance above consensus.
KLA Q3 FY2025 earnings: Revenue $3.06B (+22% YoY), EPS $8.41 (beat $8.22 estimate). Logic/foundry inspection demand robust; guided Q4 revenue $3.20B–3.50B, ahead of consensus.
Texas Instruments Q1 2025 earnings: Revenue $4.07B (+11% YoY), EPS $1.28 (beat $1.07 estimate). Analog and embedded revenue both grew; inventory correction completing. Management raised Q2 guidance.
Qualcomm Q2 FY2025 earnings: Revenue $10.84B (+16% YoY), EPS $2.85 (beat $2.80 estimate). Handsets grew 8%, Automotive hit record $959M (+59% YoY). Raised FY2025 revenue outlook.
AMD Q1 2025 earnings: Revenue $7.44B (+36% YoY), EPS $1.14 (beat $1.09 estimate). Data Center $4.1B driven by MI300X; Client and Gaming segments also beat. Q2 guidance $7.7B ± $300M.
Microsoft Q3 FY2025 earnings: Revenue $70.1B (+15% YoY), EPS $3.46 (beat $3.22 estimate). Azure grew 34% driven by AI workloads; Copilot users exceeded 16M paid seats. Strong Q4 guidance.
NVIDIA one-on-one investor meetings at JP Morgan Global Technology Leaders Forum 2025. CFO to discuss Blackwell production ramp, GB200 NVL72 rack system availability, and China compliance strategy.

Apple Analyst Day 2025 — first dedicated AI strategy briefing in 5 years. Tim Cook and SVP Software Engineering Craig Federighi to present Apple Intelligence roadmap, on-device AI chip strategy, and 2025 product cycle vision.
Micron Technology Investor Day 2025. Presenting HBM4 roadmap, CXL memory strategy, and high-capacity DRAM for AI inference servers. Production capacity update for Boise, Idaho fab expansion.

Applied Materials Investor Day 2025. CEO Gary Dickerson to present total addressable market expansion in advanced packaging, GAA transistor equipment, and AI-driven process control systems. $4B buyback program expansion expected.
ASML group of 12 equity analysts hosted for fab tour and technology briefing in Veldhoven. Deep-dive on High-NA EUV scanner yield ramp and next-generation 0.55 NA system development timeline.

NVIDIA announces a 10-for-1 forward stock split effective April 9, 2025, following prior year precedent. Post-split price approximately $88 based on pre-split price of $880. Additional shares distributed to shareholders of record as of March 28, 2025.

Broadcom completes 10-for-1 stock split on April 22, 2025. Adjusted share price of approximately $175 based on pre-split $1,750. Record date April 17, 2025. Index tracking ETFs and institutional accounts will auto-adjust.

Intel announces strategic sale of Altera FPGA business to Silverlake Partners for $8.75B. Transaction expected to close Q3 2025 pending regulatory approvals. Proceeds to fund 18A process node ramp and Intel Foundry Services expansion.

AMD acquires Nod.ai, an AI compiler startup specializing in ROCm software optimization, for $307M all-cash. Strategic acquisition closes the software stack gap vs. NVIDIA CUDA, strengthening MI-series GPU competitiveness in the open-source AI ecosystem.
Broadcom completes VMware Carbon Black security unit spin-off to Symc Holdings. Transaction valued at $2.1B. Proceeds applied toward debt repayment, reducing leverage ratio to 2.5x net debt/EBITDA.

Qualcomm announces $5B accelerated share repurchase agreement with Goldman Sachs, retiring approximately 3.2% of shares outstanding. Part of $15B buyback authorization approved in 2024; signals management confidence in AI-driven PC and auto revenue growth.

ASML announces €6B share buyback program 2025–2026, representing approximately 8% of market cap. To be funded by strong FCF generation from EUV order backlog exceeding €40B. First tranche of €1B commences May 1, 2025.
ASML Q1 2025 revenue call: CFO Roger Dassen reviews €7.74B revenue, €3.94B net bookings, and reiterates FY2025 guidance €30–35B. Q&A focus on High-NA EUV yield ramp and China export compliance.

Applied Materials Q1 FY2025 revenue call: CEO Gary Dickerson discusses $7.17B revenue, record advanced packaging orders, and raises FY2025 guidance by 3%. Analysts focus on ICAPS market recovery and AI chip equipment intensity.

Texas Instruments Q1 2025 revenue call: CFO Rafael Lizardi reviews $4.07B revenue and inventory normalization across industrial and automotive customers. Raised Q2 guidance of $4.17–4.53B signals recovery confidence.
Qualcomm Q2 FY2025 revenue call: CEO Cristiano Amon highlights record automotive revenue $959M, Snapdragon X series momentum in AI PC market, and raises Q3 revenue guidance to $11.1B–11.9B.

AMD Q1 2025 revenue call: Lisa Su discusses $7.44B revenue beat, raises FY2025 MI300X/MI350 AI GPU revenue outlook to $7.7B from prior $5.8B estimate. Client CPU market share gains in consumer and commercial segments.

Lam Research Q3 FY2025 revenue call: CEO Tim Archer discusses $4.72B revenue, NAND equipment demand acceleration, and raises Q4 guidance to $5.0B ± $300M. CSBG segment grew 19% as installed base expands.

ASML Q2 2025 guidance call: Management provides Q2 revenue outlook of €6.5–7.5B, gross margin guidance 51–53%, and reiterates FY2025 revenue target of €30–35B. CEO Christophe Fouquet highlights High-NA EUV order momentum and fab capacity expansion plans.

Lam Research Q4 FY2025 guidance call: CEO Tim Archer provides Q4 revenue outlook of $5.0B ± $300M and non-GAAP gross margin of 47.5% ± 1%. Cites accelerating NAND equipment orders and expanding CSBG service revenue as key drivers for the second half of the fiscal year.

KLA Q4 FY2025 guidance call: CEO Rick Wallace issues Q4 guidance of $3.20–3.50B revenue and EPS $8.90–10.30. Highlights rising inspection and metrology demand in advanced logic and DRAM nodes. Dividend increase of 8% to $1.70/share also announced on this call.

Texas Instruments Q2 2025 guidance call: CFO Rafael Lizardi guides Q2 revenue $4.17–4.53B and EPS $1.21–1.47, reflecting inventory normalization progress across industrial and automotive end markets. Free cash flow per share guidance maintained at $7.00–9.00 for FY2025.

Qualcomm Q3 FY2025 guidance call: CEO Cristiano Amon raises Q3 revenue outlook to $11.1–11.9B and non-GAAP EPS $2.65–2.85. Automotive design-win pipeline now exceeds $45B; IoT segment recovery on track with Edge AI platform momentum in industrial and consumer markets.

AMD Q2 2025 guidance call: CEO Lisa Su provides Q2 revenue guidance of $7.7B ± $300M, non-GAAP gross margin ~54%. Raises FY2025 AI GPU revenue outlook to $7.7B from $5.8B, driven by MI300X and upcoming MI350 ramp. Data Center segment expected to maintain double-digit sequential growth.

Microsoft Q4 FY2025 guidance call: CFO Amy Hood guides Q4 revenue $73.1–74.1B and commercial cloud revenue $45.4–45.6B. Azure growth expected to reaccelerate to 36–37% on expanded AI capacity. Copilot+PC adoption and AI services monetization to drive operating leverage in H2 FY2025.

Micron FQ2 2025 projected earnings release. Analysts estimate revenue of $8.0–8.5B driven by HBM3E shipments to AI GPU customers. EPS consensus at $1.43. DRAM pricing recovery and high-bandwidth memory mix shift are key variables; strong HBM demand from NVIDIA and AMD supply agreements in focus.

NVIDIA FQ1 2026 preliminary earnings projection. Consensus estimate: revenue $43–44B, non-GAAP EPS $0.93 (Blackwell GPU ramp). Data Center segment expected to surpass $40B for first time. Actual results expected late May; this projection reflects analyst model updates as Blackwell supply visibility improves.

ASML Q1 2025 projected earnings release. Consensus: revenue €7.6–7.9B, EPS €5.85–6.15. Net bookings expected at €3.5–4.5B with continued High-NA EUV shipments. Projection confirmed accurate — actual release scheduled Apr 16 based on early April guidance visibility.

Lam Research Q3 FY2025 projected earnings release. Analyst consensus: revenue $4.6–4.8B, EPS $0.99–1.06. NAND recovery cycle and DRAM capacity additions are primary revenue drivers; deferred revenue from upgrade and service contracts adds visibility to estimates heading into the print.

KLA Q3 FY2025 projected earnings release. Consensus estimates revenue $3.0–3.1B and EPS $8.15–8.45. Tight fab utilization and reticle inspection cycles support stable recurring revenue. Dividend sustainability and buyback pace also monitored alongside process control market share trends.

Texas Instruments Q1 2025 projected earnings release. Analyst consensus: revenue $3.95–4.10B, EPS $1.05–1.15. Industrial sector inventory draw-down and automotive demand stabilization are key upside catalysts. Analog segment gross margins watch point near 63–65%; capex trajectory for next-gen fabs also tracked.

Qualcomm Q2 FY2025 projected earnings release. Consensus: revenue $10.6–11.0B, EPS $2.77–2.88. Handset recovery, IoT segment rebound, and record automotive design-win conversion are tracked by investors. PC diversification with Snapdragon X Elite also monitored for contribution to revenue mix.

Intel Q1 2025 projected earnings release. Consensus: revenue $12.5–13.2B, EPS $0.06–0.12 (non-GAAP). Intel 18A process readiness and foundry services customer ramp are pivotal milestones. PC/server market recovery and Gaudi 3 AI accelerator adoption remain top investor focus areas this quarter.

Applied Materials Q2 FY2025 projected earnings release. Analyst estimates: revenue $7.2–7.5B, EPS $2.24–2.38. ICAPS segment (IoT, Communications, Automotive, Power, Sensors) and advanced packaging tools are demand drivers. Gate-all-around adoption by leading foundries adds further upside to estimates.

Broadcom Q2 FY2025 projected earnings release. Consensus: revenue $14.5–15.0B, non-GAAP EPS $1.48–1.56. Custom AI ASIC revenue from hyperscaler customers (Google, Meta, Apple) and VMware infrastructure software cross-sell synergies are the primary growth levers heading into this print.

AMD Q1 2025 projected earnings release. Consensus: revenue $7.1–7.5B, EPS $1.08–1.15. MI300X/MI350 AI GPU shipments and EPYC server CPU market share gains are top tracking metrics. Gaming segment headwinds partially offset by data center strength in the overall estimate.

Microsoft Q3 FY2025 projected earnings release. Consensus: revenue $68.4–70.5B, EPS $3.22–3.40. Azure AI capacity expansion and Copilot monetization ramp are key growth drivers. LinkedIn and gaming segments also monitored for contribution to the commercial cloud revenue mix.

Apple FQ2 2025 projected earnings release. Consensus: revenue $93–95B, EPS $1.56–1.64. Services segment (App Store, Apple Music, iCloud) expected to set new record above $26B. iPhone 16 cycle demand in India and Southeast Asia are incremental volume drivers; tariff impact on gross margin is a key downside risk to watch.
